metadata.notice: These placements link to source-hosted pixels and do not redistribute the source photographs. Provisional placements remain excluded from statistical alignment results until independent checkpoints are recorded.

overlays.title: Whiskey Hill 1998 source-photo registration
overlays.reference_imagery: Google Earth display of USGS imagery
overlays.review_basis: User-supplied north-up registration screenshot; source-photo-to-screenshot homography recovered from 18 inlier image features; screenshot-to-map scale anchored by the displayed formation coordinate and persistent roads.
overlays.notes: The source photo is intentionally loaded from ICCRA only after an explicit user action. The registration is visually useful but awaits independent ground-control checkpoints before acceptance.

overlays.title: Hubbard / Woodburn / Whiskey Hill 2000 close-up registration
overlays.reference_imagery: Google Earth display of USGS imagery
overlays.review_basis: The three source-photo lobe centers were matched to the three lobes visible in the user-supplied north-up historical frame; a low-tier image-axis comparison selected the threefold orientation; two manually read road controls provided an approximate local pixel-to-map scale. The calculations and limitations are persisted in data/registered_overlay_observations.json. No independent fourth ground-control point is available.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Approximate visual placement, not an accepted georegistration. Coordinate uncertainty is not independently quantified. The 35 m display-corner value is only a conditional detector-sensitivity envelope, not a confidence interval, and does not cover manual road-control error or semantic misidentification.
overlays.notes: Approximate visual-only provisional overlay. Threefold symmetry, manually read road controls, and the lack of an independent fourth checkpoint prevent acceptance as a georegistration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and loads from ICCRA only after an explicit user action.

overlays.title: Mayville / Kekoskee 2003 aerial-photo placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current satellite visual cross-check of County Road T West and the persistent field boundary
overlays.review_basis: ICCRA reports a coordinate confirmed with two GPS units, captions the aerial photograph as facing north, and records 42-foot east-west circle diameters. The source-image circle spans and southern road/field boundary were used to set a local display scale. Calculations and limitations are persisted in data/registered_overlay_observations.json.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Source-coordinate geometry placement, not a survey georegistration. The 100 m value is the conservative source-coordinate uncertainty; the photographed road boundary is a visual cross-check, not an independently surveyed ground-control point.
overlays.notes: The full ICCRA aerial frame is anchored at the source-reported GPS location and oriented from the report's north-facing caption. It loads from ICCRA only after an explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Howell Township 2003 aerial-photo placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current satellite visual cross-check of the surviving Mason Road field
overlays.review_basis: ICCRA supplies the formation GPS coordinate, identifies the Mason and Burkhardt roads field, measures the main circle at 48-52 feet, and documents opposing E/NE and W/SW pathways. The image-space pathway candidate was rotated onto that documented axis and scaled from the measured diameter.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Source-coordinate geometry placement, not a survey georegistration. The image-axis control is an interpretation of a low-tier automated candidate cross-checked against the report; no independent photographic ground checkpoint is available.
overlays.notes: The source aerial image is placed in the surviving report-identified field at the source GPS coordinate. The local scale and rotation are provisional, the image loads from ICCRA only after explicit user action, and it is excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Jupiter 2005 aerial scene placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current satellite visual review of the source-coordinate vicinity
overlays.review_basis: ICCRA gives a coordinate, measured N/S and E/W diameters, elevation, and a 20-30 foot offset from Eller Creek. The source aerial frame contains the formation, creek, tree clusters, and track crossing. The display scale is tied to the measured 57-foot oval; true image orientation is not yet independently resolved.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-anchored scene placement, not a georegistration. It identifies the source-reported site vicinity and scale, but the oblique photograph's true orientation still requires a historical orthophoto match to the creek and tree controls.
overlays.notes: This is deliberately labeled as a scene placement: it uses the actual source GPS coordinate and measured formation size but does not claim that the image corners are final. It loads from ICCRA only after explicit user action and is excluded from alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Wausau 1997 USGS follow-up scene registration
overlays.reference_imagery: Contemporaneous W-Files investigator map, Marathon County road centerlines, and current satellite imagery
overlays.review_basis: The contemporaneous W-Files investigator map places the August 13 formation east of North 41st Street and north of Townline Road. Its road grid was fitted to the present Townline/North 41st crossing, McIntosh Road, and Lahr Road. The early-1998 USGS frame independently matches the crossing, farm drive, farmstead, drainage margin, and field boundaries. The mapped symbol centroid supplies the candidate center; calculations and limitations are persisted in data/registered_overlay_observations.json.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Landmark-registered historical scene, not a survey georegistration. The contemporaneous road map locates the candidate field and persistent roads and farm features support the displayed USGS frame, but the 1997 formation is not clearly visible in that early-1998 image. The candidate center retains 100 m uncertainty and is excluded from formal alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: This is the actual ICCRA-linked USGS follow-up frame, placed over the identified Wausau field scene. It loads from ICCRA only after explicit user action; the site marker and image footprint remain provisional.

overlays.title: Wavra Farm 1997 aerial landmark placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Google Earth / USGS historical imagery
overlays.review_basis: The user supplied the actual Google Earth Pro registration and a historical-imagery screenshot. The previous Jordan Street placement is rejected: its anchor was 1,428 metres from the supplied formation anchor and did not match the highway, drives, tree rows, buildings, or field edges.
overlays.quality_disclosure: User-supplied Google Earth landmark registration. The raw KML geometry and historical-imagery date are preserved; the placement remains excluded from formal alignment calculations pending independent checkpoints.
overlays.notes: The ICCRA aerial loads only after explicit user action. This record reproduces the supplied KMZ instead of the rejected Jordan Street placement.

overlays.title: Rockville 1 2003 aerial landmark placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current Esri World Imagery, checked against the source road and parcel scene
overlays.review_basis: The named road scene and persistent intersection geometry constrain the correct field. Three non-collinear road controls replace the rejected two-point similarity fit and reproduce the visible shear of the oblique aerial frame.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine road registration, not a survey georegistration. The roads and intersection are aligned, while the crop center and outer corners retain 75 m provisional uncertainty.
overlays.notes: The source-hosted aerial loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Rockville 2 2003 campus landmark placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current Esri World Imagery of Solano Community College
overlays.review_basis: The distinctive campus, tennis courts, parking, road, and field geometry identify the scene. A two-control similarity seed supplies scale and rotation for the projective display footprint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Campus landmark scene placement, not a survey georegistration. Multiple persistent features identify the field, but the precise four-corner warp remains provisional.
overlays.notes: The actual ICCRA aerial frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Miamisburg 2004 report-coordinate image placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Historical Google Earth coordinate and alignment documented in the ICCRA investigation report
overlays.review_basis: The investigation's degree-minute-second coordinate, named mound, measured formation length, and documented alignment provide a reproducible center and display orientation.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Report-coordinate image placement, not a survey georegistration. The center and named-landmark orientation are documented; the four image corners remain a provisional reconstruction.
overlays.notes: The source-hosted formation image loads only after explicit user action and is excluded from formal alignment calculations pending independent checkpoints.

overlays.title: Hopewell / Chillicothe 2012 landmark scene placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current Esri imagery and NPS Hopewell Mound Group site geography
overlays.review_basis: Five persistent geographic controls and three report distances identify the correct field and support an explicitly provisional projective display footprint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Multi-landmark scene placement, not a surveyed registration. The field is well constrained by the report and terrain, while the precise four-corner warp remains provisional.
overlays.notes: The source-hosted aerial loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Albion / Starr Earthworks 2002 landmark scene placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Current Esri imagery and Starr Commonwealth campus geography
overlays.review_basis: The named Starr venue and distinctive triangular field bounded by two persistent transport corridors identify the scene and support a provisional footprint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Venue-field landmark placement, not a survey georegistration. The scene match is strong; precise corner geometry remains provisional.
overlays.notes: The source-hosted image loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Naperville 2002 Diehl Road field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Illinois State Geological Survey IL_CUA_2005 official orthophoto service
overlays.review_basis: The full report, named Diehl Road and Molex property, documented apartment-complex context, field acreage, source photograph, and official 1998 and 2005 Illinois orthophotos identify the former field. Three non-collinear parking/field controls reproduce the source rotation, unequal scale, and shear.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine field registration, not a survey georegistration. The correct former field is strongly supported, while the source footprint and transformed formation center retain 75 m provisional uncertainty.
overlays.notes: The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Paint Creek Island 2003 creek-boundary placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic and NPS Seip Earthworks control
overlays.review_basis: The ICCRA report, the 2003 wide aerial, NPS Seip coordinates, current imagery, and the distinctive surviving Paint Creek loop identify one field. Four non-collinear persistent island-boundary controls reproduce the oblique frame with a projective transform.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Four-control creek-island projective placement, not a survey georegistration. The field identity is strongly supported; the footprint and transformed formation center retain 100 m provisional uncertainty.
overlays.notes: The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and remains excluded from formal alignment calculations.

overlays.title: Serpent Mound / Locust Grove 2003 road-aligned field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, ICCRA 2003 mapping aerial, and contemporaneous newspaper location description
overlays.review_basis: The public ICCRA report and case PDF, the 2003 wide aerial, a contemporaneous newspaper location description, the persistent Ohio 73 and Brush Creek geometry, and current imagery identify one field. Two separated road points and the historically mapped formation anchor provide a bounded affine display fit.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Provisional road-aligned affine placement, not a survey georegistration. The unique field is well supported; the image footprint and formation center retain 100 m uncertainty and remain excluded from formal alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. The ICCRA-hosted source frame loads only after explicit user action and is not packaged by the atlas.

overlays.title: Chesterton Windmill 2018 field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, OpenStreetMap Chesterton Windmill building geometry, and the publisher map target
overlays.review_basis: The publisher map target identifies the formation center to 25 m, while the windmill building and its distinctive surrounding grass island persist in current imagery. Three non-collinear controls reproduce the source rotation, scale, and shear and place the complete near-nadir frame over the unique field.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Three-control affine field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field and source orientation are strongly supported; the footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

overlays.title: Figsbury Ring 2016 hillfort-aligned field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, the persistent Figsbury Ring earthwork, and the publisher map target
overlays.review_basis: The exact publisher map target identifies the formation center and the wide source aerial independently contains the complete persistent Figsbury Ring enclosure. Three distributed hillfort controls plus the formation anchor define a projective display fit; a held-out southern earthwork point provides an approximately 37 m residual check.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Four-control projective field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field and orientation are strongly supported; the full-frame footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

overlays.title: Yarnbury Castle 2018 earthwork-aligned field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery current mosaic, the persistent Yarnbury Castle earthwork and A303, and the publisher map target
overlays.review_basis: The exact publisher map target identifies the formation center and the wide source aerial independently contains Yarnbury Castle and the A303. Three distributed earthwork controls plus the formation anchor define a projective display fit; a held-out southeastern earthwork point has an approximately 13 m residual and the transformed road follows the reference A303.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Four-control projective field registration, not a survey georegistration. The unique field, earthwork, and road orientation are strongly supported; the full-frame footprint remains provisional and excluded from formal alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

overlays.title: Coneybury Hill 2018 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 40 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 40 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring cardinal points define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification around that target. Same-flight wide frames and the paired copses support the selected orientation and unique field, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials place the two persistent copses on the source horizontal axis; the tight frame's tramline direction is consistent with that scene orientation.

overlays.title: Cley Hill 2016 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 43 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 43 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification around that target. Same-flight wide frames, Cley Hill, the field boundary, and the matching tramline direction support the selected field and orientation, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials resolve the formation field immediately south of Cley Hill. The tight frame's tramline axis is aligned to the matching persistent southeast field direction in the reference mosaic.

overlays.title: Cocum Farm 2019 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 61 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 61 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames and the persistent tree avenue, hedge junction, road, and farm context identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial places the long Cocum Farm tree avenue directly north of the formation and preserves the tight frame's star and tramline orientation, fixing image-up approximately to true north.

overlays.title: Kitt's Lane 2021 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG Streetmap target, documented 55 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a BNG target with 25 metre atlas uncertainty and reports an approximately 55 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames and persistent woodland, buildings, hedges, and field corners identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials match the woodland block, Kitt's Lane building cluster, hedgerows, and field corners; the near-nadir frame is rotated so image-up points approximately west-northwest toward that woodland context.

overlays.title: Clear Wood 2019 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 55 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and reports an approximately 55 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames and the persistent road, field boundaries, woodland edge, and Cley Hill context identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials resolve the road, Clear Wood, field corners, and Cley Hill context. The selected frame's bubble axis points toward the road side of the field, fixing image-up approximately north within the stated uncertainty.

overlays.title: Etchilhampton 2016 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG Streetmap target, reported 24 metre parenthetical diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a BNG map target with 25 metre atlas uncertainty and reports c.89 feet (24m). Four visible outer-ring cardinal controls define a coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained crop-geometry placement around that target. The same-flight wide frame and persistent field outline, village, roads, hedges, and matching crop-row direction identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial matches the uniquely shaped field west of Etchilhampton village, the eastern road, northern hedge, and adjoining fields. Its crop-row direction transfers the near-nadir frame so image-up points approximately west-northwest.

overlays.title: The Ridgeway / Hackpen White Horse 2020 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG target, documented 33.5 metre diameter, same-flight landmark panorama, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a ten-digit BNG target and reports c.110 feet (33.5m). Four visible outer-circle controls define a crop-geometry rectification around that target. The tight frame is linked to a same-flight landmark panorama by 511 SIFT/RANSAC inliers with 0.75 pixel median and 1.88 pixel p95 residual, while the panorama matches the oval copse, track, road loop, pasture, and parcel edges. Those data establish the field and yaw quadrant, but the four scale controls remain formation geometry rather than independent persistent ground checkpoints.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: A same-flight panorama transfers the tight-frame tramline axis through 511 robust local-feature inliers and matches the oval copse, Ridgeway track, road loop, white-horse pasture, and parcel edges. The resulting undirected field axis is approximately 23.5 degrees true.

overlays.title: Roundway Hill 2021 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG target, documented 49 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a BNG target with 25 metre atlas uncertainty and reports c.160 feet (49m). Four visible outer-circle controls define a projective crop-geometry rectification around that target. Same-flight wide frames reproduce the distinctive field network and southwest field corner and constrain the row axis, but no single source frame supplies four distributed persistent projective controls or three independent affine controls.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the distinctive field network and southwest field corner. The selected frame's nearly horizontal tramlines transfer to an approximately 83-degree true undirected row axis in the north-up reference mosaic.

overlays.title: Ansty 2016 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG target, Temporary Temples approximate 450-foot diameter, source aerial landmarks, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies BNG E=395096 N=125522 with 25 metre atlas uncertainty. Temporary Temples reports an approximately 450-foot diameter. Four visible outer-circle controls define the crop-geometry placement around the publisher target. The source and reference scenes reproduce the nursery, road and compound, field boundaries, and isolated shed; the shed contributes to the orientation choice and therefore is not an independent checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The source-to-reference vectors from the formation center to the persistent isolated southeastern shed, together with the nursery, road, compound, and field boundaries, orient image-up approximately 19 degrees east of true north.

overlays.title: Ranscomb Bottom 2016 coordinate-size geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG target, reported 177 metre overall size, wider source aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies BNG E=403970 N=168308 with 25 metre atlas uncertainty and reports c.580 feet (177m) overall. Four formation-extremity points constrain display scale and skew around that target. Wider frames independently identify the hook-shaped field and surrounding landscape and support the approximate undirected field-row axis, but the tight frame contains no independently identifiable persistent geographic controls.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Wider aerials identify the unique hook-shaped field. The tight frame's tramlines are aligned to the approximate undirected row axis visible in the current reference field; image-up is estimated about 7 degrees west of true north.

overlays.title: Darfield 2002 legacy-coordinate and measured-scale placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Two user-supplied legacy KML points, Crop Circle Research Archive historical Google Earth frame, the matching 2002 catalog diagram, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The formation identity is fixed by the unique 2002 diagram match. The measured source-frame scale bar and publisher-stated north-up orientation constrain the display footprint around the northern legacy point, whose approximately 85 metre road offset matches the source frame better than the southern point. The coordinate conflict is retained as 50 metre uncertainty and no persistent geographic checkpoint independently validates the full-frame corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, measured-scale-, and publisher-orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. The Google Earth-derived source frame is not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and supporting source link only.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

overlays.review_basis: The legacy point, matching case photograph, historical frame, measured scale bar, and publisher-stated north-up constrain a display footprint. The event-level date disagreement is retained and no persistent landmark independently validates the frame corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, measured-scale-, and publisher-orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. The Google Earth-derived source frame is not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and supporting source links only.

overlays.title: Panocchia 2004 legacy-coordinate and reported-size placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Two user-supplied legacy KML points, Google Sightseeing historical Google Maps thumbnail and map URL, corroborating 2004 reports and diagram, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The event identity is fixed by the three-circle design and corroborating 2004 reports. The source map URL repeats the preferred coordinate, while the 71.735 metre conflicting KML point remains separate and sets the uncertainty. Reported circle size and north-up source presentation constrain only a provisional display footprint.

overlays.title: Hackpen Hill 2003 legacy-coordinate and measured-scale placement
overlays.reference_imagery: User-supplied legacy KML point, Crop Circle Research Archive historical Google Earth frame, matching corn664 aerial photograph, and COMBINED catalog diagram
overlays.review_basis: The design-specific source aerial, catalog diagram, archive code, and ghost fix the event identity. The legacy coordinate, measured scale bar, and publisher-stated north-up constrain a display footprint, but no persistent landmark independently validates its corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, measured-scale-, and publisher-orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and is not a real-world accuracy benchmark.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: coordinate_size_geometry_provisional. The Google Earth-derived source frame and linked aerial are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and supporting source links only.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

overlays.title: Huntingburg 2006 airport-grove field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Indiana Geographic Information Office 2005 Orthophotography
overlays.review_basis: The field is uniquely identified by four persistent scene classes visible together in the source and official near-contemporaneous orthophoto: runway, hangars, access road, and grove. Seven controls produce a visually coherent projective fit and the held-out airport checkpoint residual is 32.35 metres.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Real historical-image field identification with a provisional seven-control projective display fit. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and cannot become accepted or publication-eligible without independent human review.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Earthfiles retains all source-image rights; the atlas publishes the reviewed footprint and source link only and neither packages nor embeds the source pixels.

overlays.title: The Belt 2015 road-and-woodland field placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery
overlays.review_basis: The publisher coordinate and four persistent scene classes identify one field. Eight boundary controls produce a coherent, non-folded display transform with 20.84 metre control-fit RMSE; this fit residual is not an independent accuracy result.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Real historical-image field identification with a provisional eight-control projective display fit. It is excluded from formal alignment calculations and cannot become accepted or publication-eligible without independent human review.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Crop Circle Connector contributors retain the source-image rights; the atlas publishes the reviewed footprint and source link only and neither packages nor embeds the source pixels.

overlays.title: Mixon 2018 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher BNG target, reported 91.5 metre overall size, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a ten-digit BNG target and reports a 300-foot (91.5 metre) overall size. Four visible formation extremities constrain the display footprint around that target. Same-flight wide frames identify the target field and approximate undirected row axis through the village edge, hedges, road junction, and field outline, but no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement, not an independently validated image-to-ground registration or real-world accuracy benchmark. It remains excluded from formal alignment calculations and cannot become accepted without independent human review.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Crop Circle Connector contributors retain the source-image rights; the atlas publishes the reviewed footprint and source link only and neither packages nor embeds the source pixels.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the large field west of Mixon village, the village-edge hedges, road junction, and surrounding parcel geometry. The tight frame's horizontal tramlines are aligned to the approximately 154-degree true undirected row axis estimated from that context and the current reference field.

overlays.title: Lion's Gate / Etchilhampton Hill 2024 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Two independent publisher BNG targets, Temporary Temples reported size, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: Two precise publisher map targets resolve one narrow field, while the same-flight wide aerial identifies the surrounding field boundaries and transfers the approximate row-axis orientation to the tight frame through 53 RANSAC inliers. Four formation extremities constrain the footprint at the midpoint of the documented size range; no persistent ground point independently validates its corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Real historical-image field identification with a provisional coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display footprint. It is not a real-world accuracy benchmark, remains excluded from formal alignment calculations, and cannot become accepted or publication-eligible without independent human review.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Crop Circle Connector and Temporary Temples contributors retain the source-image rights; the atlas publishes the reviewed footprint and source links only and neither packages nor embeds the source pixels.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The tight frame is linked to the same-flight wide aerial by 53 RANSAC inliers from 91 ratio-test matches. Village-edge and field-boundary context in the wide frame, together with the north-up reference field rows, places the tight frame's positive x-axis approximately north; a 180-degree ambiguity remains.

overlays.title: Gurston Ashes 2018 dual-DJI camera-pose placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Two source-hosted DJI frames with embedded camera pose, the publisher BNG target, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The primary full-frame Mavic image retains a complete DJI pose and calibration record. Its image-derived formation center agrees within 3.16 metres with the sub-camera point of a second, near-vertical DJI FC6310 frame made by another contributor. The surrounding field and woodland geometry agree with the publisher field and current reference imagery.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Real historical-image camera-pose registration with a 3.16 metre cross-photo pose check, not a survey georegistration or a real-world accuracy benchmark. It remains provisional, excluded from formal alignment calculations, and cannot become accepted or publication-eligible without independent human review.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Crop Circle Connector contributors retain the source-image rights; the atlas publishes the reviewed footprint and source links only and neither packages nor embeds the source pixels.

overlays.title: Hackpen Hill 2018 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 27.5 metre diameter, same-flight wide aerial context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre coordinate target and an approximately 27.5 metre diameter. Four visible outer-ring controls define a tight projective crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight context and persistent field/downland geometry identify the unique field and approximate yaw, but the tight source frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It is useful for display and visual comparison only and remains excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide frame fixes the field against the Hackpen downland, road, pasture, and tree clumps. The selected frame's principal tramline axis is aligned to the corresponding field direction in the current reference mosaic.

overlays.title: Cley Hill 2017 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented minimum 92 metre diameter, same-flight Cley Hill context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre target and reports an overall diameter in excess of 92 metres. Four visible outer-scallop controls define the tight crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide frames identify the field and yaw quadrant, but the tight frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint and 92 metres is treated as the documented minimum size.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Minimum-documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It remains provisional and excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide frames reproduce Cley Hill, the field boundary, and the adjoining field. The selected frame's tramline family is aligned to the corresponding field direction, with a conservative yaw uncertainty.

overlays.title: Cley Hill 2020 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 30.5 metre size, same-flight and hilltop Cley Hill context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre target and an approximately 30.5 metre size. Four reviewed outer-ring controls define the crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight and hilltop context frames resolve the field and approximate yaw, but no independent ground checkpoint validates the tight-frame corners.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It remains provisional and excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The wide drone and hilltop context frames reproduce the Cley Hill slope, curved crop boundary, and woodland. The tight frame's tramline direction is aligned to that field context with a conservative yaw uncertainty.

overlays.title: Battlebury Hill 2017 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 61 by 61 metre size, same-flight Battlebury Hill context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre target and an approximately 61 by 61 metre size. Four outer-loop controls define the crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight wide context resolves the field and approximate yaw, but the tight frame contains no independent persistent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It remains provisional and excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial fixes the field relative to wooded Battlebury Hill, the hill track, and adjacent field boundaries. The selected frame's tramlines are aligned to the corresponding field direction in the current mosaic.

overlays.title: Thorn Hill 2017 documented-size crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Publisher Streetmap target, documented 36.5 by 18.25 metre extent, same-flight Thorn Hill context, and Esri World Imagery current mosaic
overlays.review_basis: The publisher supplies a 25 metre target, 36.5 metre overall length, and 18.25 metre largest-circle diameter. Four formation-bound controls define the tight crop-geometry rectification. Same-flight context resolves the field and approximate end-to-end direction, but the tight frame contains no independent ground checkpoint.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Documented-size crop-geometry rectification, not a real-world accuracy benchmark or survey georegistration. It remains provisional and excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Explicit outcome: provisional_registration. Contributor-retained source pixels are not embedded or redistributed; the public atlas exposes the reviewed footprint and source link only.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial fixes the long field between the eastern lane and southern woodland belt. The hollow-ring end is the southern end and the selected frame's left-to-right formation axis is mapped north-northwest along the field rows.

overlays.title: Diessenhofen 2008-07-15 16:44:08 landmark-controlled aerial placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery
overlays.review_basis: Manual correspondences on four persistent boundaries surrounding the formation field, checked against the Rhine, tree lines, buildings, farm tracks, and southern transport corridor in the wider source scene.
overlays.rights_attribution: Hansueli Krapf / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0
overlays.quality_disclosure: The correct Diessenhofen field is strongly supported by the multi-landmark scene, but the displayed four-corner warp remains provisional and must not be used as evidence for long-distance alignments.
overlays.notes: Openly licensed source pixels load only after explicit user action. The placement is excluded from formal alignment calculations.

transform.basis: Manual correspondences on four persistent boundaries surrounding the formation field, checked against the Rhine, tree lines, buildings, farm tracks, and southern transport corridor in the wider source scene.

scene_identity_evidence: Rhine river channel and both bank alignments
road and building group north of the river
isolated tree cluster and green wedge immediately northwest of the formation field
west crop boundary and east farm-track boundary
southern railway/road corridor, including the train visible in the source frame

overlays.title: Diessenhofen 2008-07-15 16:44:20 same-flight aerial placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery
overlays.review_basis: Same-flight transfer to the accepted reference scene plus a manual formation-center constraint.
overlays.rights_attribution: Hansueli Krapf / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0
overlays.quality_disclosure: This same-flight frame uses a constrained SIFT similarity transfer (5 RANSAC inliers on its final edge) from the reviewed Diessenhofen reference frame. The formation center was visually checked, but oblique parallax, extrapolated frame corners, and the absence of independent ground checkpoints make the display warp provisional and exclude it from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: Openly licensed source pixels load only after explicit user action. This placement is display-only.

overlays.title: Hörhausen 2009 open-license aerial crop-geometry placement
overlays.reference_imagery: Esri World Imagery plus Wikimedia Commons geotags
overlays.review_basis: Same-event later documentation, two nearby open-license geotags, a documented 120 metre size, and field-row orientation constrain a useful display placement without claiming persistent-landmark validation.
overlays.quality_disclosure: Provisional crop-geometry rectification, not an exact landmark georegistration. Event identity and field vicinity are well supported; the precise center, rotation, scale, and four projected corners remain uncertain and are excluded from alignment calculations.
overlays.notes: The CC BY 2.0 aerial loads from Wikimedia Commons only after explicit user action. The related ground photograph is linked in the report image archive. This display placement cannot originate or validate an alignment ray.

quality_gate.reason: crop geometry and approximate geotags are not independent persistent ground control
